records.ety: [OE. alien, OF. alier, F. alier, fr. L. alligare to bind to; ad + ligare to bind. Cf. Alligate, Alloy, Allay, Ligament.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Allied (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Allying.]

defs: To unite, or form a connection between, as between families by marriage, or between princes and states by treaty, league, or confederacy; -- often followed by to or with.
quotes: O chief! in blood, and now in arms allied.

defs: To connect or form a relation between by similitude, resemblance, friendship, or love.
quotes: These three did love each other dearly well, And with so firm affection were allied.
The virtue nearest to our vice allied.
notes: ☞ Ally is generally used in the passive form or reflexively.

defs: One united to another by treaty or league; -- usually applied to sovereigns or states; a confederate.
quotes: The English soldiers and their French allies.

defs: Anything associated with another as a helper; an auxiliary.
quotes: Science, instead of being the enemy of religion, becomes its ally.

defs: Anything akin to another by structure, etc.
See Alley, a marble or taw.
